nodes.label: What is reviewed, by hand, on purpose
nodes.norm_label: what is reviewed, by hand, on purpose

nodes.label: Availability Model
nodes.rationale: Every symbol resolves to Availability with added, deprecated, removed, replacement, extension and compiler_optimized facts.

nodes.label: Version-Aware Query API
nodes.rationale: The README groups per-symbol lookups, per-version sets, diffs, batch reports, callable methods and inventory queries as the consumer-facing query layer.

nodes.label: Symbol Case Rules
nodes.rationale: Functions, classes and methods are matched case-insensitively, while constants are case-sensitive.

nodes.label: Batch Compatibility Report
nodes.rationale: The README describes compatibility_issue_at and compatibility_report_at as structured checks for symbols resolved by another tool.

nodes.label: Callable Method Lookup
nodes.rationale: The README distinguishes declared-only method lookup from callable_method_availability, which follows parents and interfaces.

nodes.label: Inventory And Trust APIs
nodes.rationale: The README names canonical resolution, extension inventory, source_manifest, supported_versions and availability_provenance as trust-facing APIs.

nodes.label: Optional Serde Feature
nodes.rationale: Serde support is an optional feature and is off by default.

nodes.label: Mago Consumer Model
nodes.rationale: The expansion spec frames Mago as a Rust AST consumer that wants this crate to answer PHP version compatibility facts.

nodes.label: Change-Set Queries
nodes.rationale: The spec proposes function, constant, class and method change queries between two PHP versions.

nodes.label: As-Of Reverse Iterators
nodes.rationale: The spec defines added_in, deprecated_as_of and removed_by iterators for each symbol kind.

nodes.label: Canonical Name Resolution
nodes.rationale: The spec defines resolve_* APIs so consumers can map source spelling to canonical table keys.

nodes.label: Supported Versions And Source Manifest
nodes.rationale: The spec asks for supported_versions, coverage_range and source_manifest to expose the fact base and provenance.

nodes.label: Extension Inventory
nodes.rationale: The spec defines extension listing and per-symbol extension requirement APIs for consumer rules.

nodes.label: Compatibility Queries
nodes.rationale: The spec defines compatibility_issue_at, compatibility_report_at and compatibility_window for version diagnostics.

nodes.label: Callable Method Resolution
nodes.rationale: The spec treats inherited method lookup as a new API separate from declared-only method semantics.

nodes.label: Availability Provenance
nodes.rationale: The spec defines provenance and confidence metadata so consumers can explain why compatibility facts are trusted.

nodes.label: Serde Support
nodes.rationale: The spec requires serde to be optional and disabled by default.

nodes.label: Offline Regeneration Tool
nodes.rationale: The regeneration README describes a developer-only offline generator that rewrites generated tables.

nodes.label: Pinned Upstream Sources
nodes.rationale: The generator reads pinned phpstorm-stubs, PHPCompatibility and PHP-CS-Fixer inputs.

nodes.label: Regeneration Drift Gates
nodes.rationale: Generation failures force review when upstream sources disagree, disappear, or change format.

nodes.label: Rust CI Quality Gates
nodes.rationale: CI runs cargo fmt, cargo clippy with warnings denied and cargo test on pushes and pull requests.

nodes.label: Graphify Workflow
nodes.rationale: AGENTS.md instructs codebase questions to use the existing graph before broad source browsing.

nodes.label: Architecture Guidance
nodes.rationale: CLAUDE.md separates the library from the generator and emphasises permissive data sourcing.
